label: Zimmerman (2002)
apa7: Zimmerman, B. J. (2002). Becoming a self-regulated learner: An overview. Theory Into Practice, 41(2), 64–70. https://doi.org/10.1207/s15430421tip4102_2
note: Thesis referenziert gelegentlich als Zimmerman (2000) – korrekt ist 2002. Zimmerman (2000) ist ein separates Buchkapitel im Handbook of Self-Regulation.

forethought_phase.description: Processes and beliefs that occur BEFORE efforts to learn
forethought_phase.thesis_mapping: F3_nonstarter: Non-Starter initiieren die Forethought Phase nicht – kein Lernziel, keine Strategie, kein Zyklus-Eintritt. Dies ist der zentrale Mechanismus hinter TF2.

performance_phase.description: Processes that occur DURING behavioral implementation
performance_phase.thesis_mapping: Nicht direkt in Features operationalisiert – aber Hintergrund für warum Studienaktivität (Einsendungen) als Proxy für Performance Phase dient

self_reflection_phase.description: Processes that occur AFTER each learning effort
self_reflection_phase.thesis_mapping: Defensive Reaktionen (Kursabbruch, Nicht-Engagement) als mögliche Manifestation bei Frühkündigern

key_definitions.definition: Self-regulation refers to self-generated thoughts, feelings, and behaviors that are oriented to attaining goals
Self-regulation is not a mental ability or an academic performance skill; rather it is the self-directive process by which learners transform their mental abilities into academic skills
Novices fail to engage in high-quality forethought and instead attempt to self-regulate their learning reactively. That is, they fail to set specific goals or to self-monitor systematically
key_definitions.thesis_mapping: Non-Starter als Extremfall von Novizen – keinerlei Forethought-Aktivität

key_arguments.argument: Self-regulation is not a single personal trait that individual students either possess or lack. Instead, it involves the selective use of specific processes that must be personally adapted to each learning task
The forethought phase refers to processes and beliefs that occur before efforts to learn
Task analysis involves goal setting and strategic planning. There is considerable evidence of increased academic success by learners who set specific proximal goals for themselves
This view of self-regulation is cyclical in that self-reflections from prior efforts to learn affect subsequent forethought processes
key_arguments.thesis_mapping: Erklärt warum frühe Inaktivität (kein Einstieg) sich selbst verstärkt

key_arguments.argument: Novices fail to engage in high-quality forethought and instead attempt to self-regulate their learning reactively – they fail to set specific goals or to self-monitor systematically
Self-motivated quality of self-regulated learners depends on several underlying beliefs, including perceived efficacy and intrinsic interest

direct_quotes.quote: Self-regulation refers to self-generated thoughts, feelings, and behaviors that are oriented to attaining goals
direct_quotes.note: Zimmerman verweist hier auf Zimmerman (2000) – das ist sein Buchkapitel, NICHT dieser Artikel

direct_quotes.quote: Self-regulation is not a mental ability or an academic performance skill; rather it is the self-directive process by which learners transform their mental abilities into academic skills

The forethought phase refers to processes and beliefs that occur before efforts to learn; the performance phase refers to processes that occur during behavioral implementation, and self-reflection refers to processes that occur after each learning effort
Novices fail to engage in high-quality forethought and instead attempt to self-regulate their learning reactively. That is, they fail to set specific goals or to self-monitor systematically
direct_quotes.thesis_mapping: Direkter Bezug zu F3_nonstarter als Manifestation fehlender Forethought

figure_1.description: Phases and Subprocesses of Self-Regulation – zyklisches Drei-Phasen-Modell
figure_1.note: Abbildung zeigt Forethought, Performance und Self-Reflection als Kreislauf mit Pfeilen

important_distinction.zimmerman_2000_vs_2002: Zimmerman (2000) ist: Attainment of self-regulation: A social cognitive perspective. In Boekaerts, Pintrich & Zeidner (Eds.), Handbook of self-regulation, pp. 13–39. Academic Press. – Ein SEPARATES Werk. Zimmerman (2002) ist DIESER Artikel in Theory Into Practice.
important_distinction.page_reference: S. 65 Fußnote: Zimmerman (2000) wird im Text selbst als separate Quelle zitiert

common_errors: Zimmerman (2000) wenn dieser Artikel gemeint ist – FALSCH, korrekt: Zimmerman (2002)
Zimmerman (2002, S. 67–68) für die Phasen – korrekt wenn aus diesem Artikel zitiert
Zimmerman (2000) für das Drei-Phasen-Modell – NUR korrekt wenn das Buchkapitel gemeint ist, nicht dieser Artikel
